summary: compactListFields must keep this CLI's primary record fields (headline/link/source/num/topic/rank/...) and must never collapse a record to an empty object, so --agent (which implies --compact) returns usable data.
reason: Techmeme's headline/river/trending records key their content off domain fields outside the generic id/name/title allow-list, so the stock --compact path stripped every field and `search <topic> --agent` returned a list of empty {} objects -- unusable for any agent or downstream source integration.
validated_outcome: After the fix, `techmeme-pp-cli search "AI" --agent` returns 16 records each carrying headline/link/source/num instead of {}; full module test suite passes (92 tests).
upstream_issue: The empty-object-on-compact behavior is generator-shaped (compactListFields is generated boilerplate); the allow-list gap and the no-blank guard should also land in cli-printing-press templates so other headline/link-shaped CLIs do not regress.
